whatThisIs: Search the NLM RxNorm vocabulary - the U.S. standard for naming clinical drugs across brand, generic, ingredient, strength, and dose-form combinations. RxNorm assigns an RXCUI (a stable concept identifier) to each distinct drug concept and links the synonyms together, so "Lipitor 20 mg oral tablet", "atorvastatin 20 mg oral tablet", and the underlying ingredient "atorvastatin" all resolve to a connected graph. Sophie Well lets you type an ingredient, a brand, or a partial string and shows the matching concepts with their RXCUIs.
whenToUse: Use this when an interface message arrives with an RXCUI you do not recognize, when reconciling a medication list across two systems that disagree on drug names, or when verifying which RXCUI represents the ingredient versus the specific clinical drug form. RxNorm is the lingua franca for medication exchange between EHRs, pharmacy systems, and payers, so confirming the right concept identifier is the difference between a clean interface and a downstream mapping error that propagates silently.
